fields.description: Collecting per-container and per-node resource consumption statistics in a fairly granular manner, and making them available to both infrastructure code (e.g., schedulers) and users (e.g., AMs or directly users via webapps), can facilitate several performance work.

comments.body: Credit to [~gopalv] for the YARN "top" idea.
I have been meaning to take a stab at it for quite sometime. I should be able to start working on this in a week or two.

This is great.. feel free to reach out to me if you care to discuss this... We have been thinking about it for few use cases, it might help to have a quick chat to make sure the way you structure covers them all.

Filed YARN-2984 to capture container's actual memory consumption. Will file another sub-task for CPU (Carlo has emailed me his implementation offline). 

YARN-2965 covers capturing node resource consumption.
Related to and very likely a dup of YARN-1012 which is part of a larger effort.
One other related effort is YARN-2928 which is also planning to obtain and send information about container resource-usage to a per-application aggregator. We should try to unify these..
I would like to look at YARN-2141 as a pre-requisite of YARN-1012. Once we capture the actual resource consumption, we can use it in multiple ways - (1) Send to the RM, (2) Log it for debugging etc.
[~curino] - the two sub-tasks capture memory and CPU in NM metrics. Did you have anything else in mind to be done here? Otherwise, we could mark this as resolved.

bq. One other related effort is YARN-2928 which is also planning to obtain and send information about container resource-usage to a per-application aggregator. We should try to unify these..
Filed YARN-3332.
Karthik, it would be very useful to grab also disk and network consumption information. I believe the mechanisms are very similar, it should be easy.

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open
